pairs.question: What were the main professional roles of Arne Falstad throughout his career?
pairs.answer: Arne Falstad held multiple roles including attorney, banker, politician, and military officer. He worked as a junior solicitor and later attorney in Steinkjer from 1903 to 1921, served as director of Privatbanken in Levanger from 1921 to 1928, and practiced law in Levanger thereafter. He also served on municipal councils, including as mayor of Levanger Municipality, and as a deputy representative in the Parliament of Norway.

pairs.question: What military ranks did Arne Falstad achieve during his service?
pairs.answer: Arne Falstad was a conscript officer, achieving the rank of Premier Lieutenant in 1902 and Captain in 1911.

pairs.question: How did Arne Falstad contribute to Norwegian politics beyond his parliamentary role?
pairs.answer: Beyond serving as a deputy representative in the Parliament of Norway, Falstad was actively involved in local politics. He was a member of the executive committee of Steinkjer city council from 1913 to 1919, serving as deputy mayor from 1913 to 1916. He later served on the municipal council of Levanger Municipality from 1928 to 1940, holding the positions of deputy mayor and mayor multiple times.

pairs.question: What educational background did Arne Falstad have?
pairs.answer: Arne Falstad completed his secondary education in Trondhjem in 1892 and graduated with a cand.jur. degree in 1900.

pairs.question: What significant bank leadership roles did Arne Falstad undertake?
pairs.answer: Falstad served as director of Privatbanken in Levanger from 1921 to 1928 and chaired Indherred Kreditbank.

pairs.question: When and where was Arne Falstad born, and who were his parents?
pairs.answer: Arne Falstad was born on 1 May 1874 at Skjerpengen in Skogn Municipality. He was the son of banker Peder Falstad (1842–1921) and his wife Oline Kathrine Sæther (1837–1928).

pairs.question: What were Arne Falstad's family connections to politics?
pairs.answer: Arne Falstad was a maternal grandson of politician Peter Andreas Sæther.
